diagrams.title: Basic Logic Gates Symbols
diagrams.description: Standard symbols for the three basic logic gates: AND, OR, and NOT
elements: AND gate: D-shaped with flat side on input, curved output
OR gate: Shield-shaped with curved input side, pointed output
NOT gate: Triangle with small circle at tip (inversion bubble)
diagrams.purpose: Learn standard IEEE/ANSI symbols for basic logic gates used in digital circuit diagrams

diagrams.title: Truth Table for Basic Gates
diagrams.description: Complete truth tables showing output for all input combinations
diagrams.purpose: Understand functional behavior of basic gates through complete input-output mapping

diagrams.title: Digital vs Analog Signal Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing discrete digital signals versus continuous analog signals
elements: Digital: Square wave with only two levels (0 and 1)
Analog: Smooth sinusoidal wave with continuous varying values
Threshold boundaries shown for digital signal detection
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the fundamental difference between digital (discrete) and analog (continuous) signal representations
